score.label: Major Work Needed
score.interpretation: Major work needed — multiple stages breaking

diagnosis.headline: Pain resonates, but buyers cannot place LabV, justify the switch, or take a safe first step
diagnosis.text: Outbound will generate curiosity because 'data chaos' is a recognized pain. But prospects will stall after checking the homepage: they cannot tell what category LabV belongs to, proof does not match the target buyer role, and there is no low-commitment entry point.

demo_conversion.basis: Based on Q1 (category unclear) + Q11-12 (proof gaps)

internal_sell.basis: Based on Q22 (no ROI) + Q14 (role mismatch)

pipeline_speed.basis: Based on Q5 (no urgency) + Q17 (no safe entry)

predictions.summary: Buyers will respond to pain-based messaging but stall when evaluating fit and building internal consensus.

recognition: So is this like a LIMS? How is it different?
We already have a data management system. Why would we switch?
This sounds like a big project. What's the minimum commitment?
Who else in our industry uses this? Can we talk to them?

message_priority.evidence: Buyers scanning the hero see category claim first, not their pain. They must scroll to find relevance. Outbound must invert this order.

buyer_focus.evidence: 'R&D teams' is broad. Buyers cannot quickly tell if this is for their industry or team size.

why_act_now.evidence: Pain named but no trigger moment. Buyers cannot identify when this becomes urgent. They can defer indefinitely.

proof_relevance.evidence: VARTA and NETZSCH named, but many testimonials anonymous. Metrics show '0%'. No quotes from Head of R&D role.

explanation_burden.evidence: 'Material Intelligence Platform' is invented. Buyers cannot place it in a known category. Sales must explain from scratch.

internal_sharing.evidence: If a champion forwards this link, the next reader will struggle to understand value without context. No ROI framework visible.

mixed_signals.evidence: Enterprise testimonials (VARTA) but no enterprise buying path (security page, compliance badges). 'Start within three months' suggests enterprise timeline but CTA is generic 'Request Demo.'

breakpoint.insight: Buyers understand the pain ('data chaos') but cannot connect it to their own project or urgency. The homepage describes what LabV does but never names the buyer's task or the cost of waiting.

missing_items.text: What project this serves (e.g., 'preparing for scale-up')
Cost of inaction if they wait another 6 months
Quantified results from customers like them
A safe first step with low commitment

decisions.name: Category
decisions.finding: 'Material Intelligence Platform' is invented. Buyers search for 'LIMS alternative' or 'R&D data management.'

decisions.name: Alternative
decisions.finding: Excel named. LIMS never addressed. 'We already have a LIMS' objection will surface on every call.

decisions.name: Why Act Now
decisions.finding: Pain named but cost of inaction absent. No 'this costs you X hours/month' or timing pressure.

decisions.name: First Step
decisions.finding: 'Request Demo' exists but no pilot, trial, or specifics. High-commitment CTA with no risk reversal.

recommendations.action: Buying Path Readiness Sprint before outbound begins

goals.label: Define Category
goals.description: Position against a buyer-recognized bucket — 'LIMS alternative for formulation teams' or own 'R&D operating system' with clear boundaries.

goals.label: Define Alternative
goals.description: Name LIMS explicitly. 'Unlike traditional LIMS...' or 'If you've outgrown Excel but LIMS feels like overkill...'

goals.label: Define Why Now
goals.description: Quantify cost of inaction. 'Every month without connected data costs X hours of repeated work.'

goals.label: Define First Step
goals.description: Replace 'Request Demo' with lower-commitment offer. 'See LabV in 15 minutes' or offer a scoped pilot.

evidence.question: What is this?
evidence.inference: Invented category. Buyers cannot file this.

evidence.question: What do you do?
evidence.quote: brings formulations, processes and test data together

evidence.question: What project?
evidence.inference: Product-centric framing. No buyer task referenced.

evidence.question: Why act now?
evidence.quote: takes time without contributing to development

evidence.question: For my team?
evidence.inference: Too broad. Industries implied but not stated.

evidence.question: For my situation?
evidence.quote: small teams as well as complex environments

evidence.question: Switching from?
evidence.quote: replaces Excel chaos and disconnected tools

evidence.question: Why alternatives fail?
evidence.inference: Excel weakness stated. LIMS never addressed.

evidence.question: What's different?
evidence.inference: Generic. Not tied to competitor weakness.

evidence.question: Proof match role?
evidence.inference: Mixed roles. No Head of R&D quotes.

evidence.question: Enterprise signals?
evidence.inference: Some compliance. No security page visible.

evidence.question: Offer match motion?
evidence.inference: Mixed signals. No enterprise buying path.

indicators.what: 'What is this?' asked in sales calls
Buyer accurately restates value after homepage visit
indicators.direction: Should increase after message priority fix
Should improve as proof match strengthens

indicators.what: Time from first touch to internal champion
indicators.direction: Should shorten as internal sharing improves
